name: Pineapple Jam Biscuits
description: Round butter biscuits with pineapple jam filling, a staple during festive seasons and a beloved traditional treat across generations.

name: Ais Gems
description: Tiny round biscuits topped with colourful hard icing in pastel shades. A childhood favourite often eaten by biting off the icing first.

name: Haw Flakes
description: Thin disc-shaped sweets made from hawthorn fruit, stacked in a cylindrical wrapper. Known for their distinctive tangy-sweet taste.

name: Love Letters (Kueh Kapit)
description: Thin crispy egg-based rolled wafers with a delicate coconut flavour. Traditionally handmade over a charcoal mould during festive seasons.

name: White Rabbit Candy
description: Iconic milk candy wrapped in edible rice paper. Originally from Shanghai, it became a beloved treat across Southeast Asia.

name: Thailand Pineapple
description: Small golden biscuit shaped like a pineapple, often enjoyed as a nostalgic sweet treat during tea time or festive gatherings.

name: Chocolate Wafer Stick
description: Long, thin crispy wafer sticks filled with chocolate cream, commonly shared as a nostalgic childhood snack during breaks or after school.

name: Thick Chocolate Waffle
description: Thick waffle-shaped biscuit with a classic grid pattern, often enjoyed as a nostalgic crunchy snack during tea time.

name: Chocolate Wafer
description: Light rectangular layered wafers with chocolate cream filling, a nostalgic snack often eaten during school breaks or afternoon tea.

name: Strawberry Wafer
description: Crispy rectangular wafers with pink strawberry cream layers, bringing back memories of sweet childhood snack times.

name: Chocolate Roll
description: Crunchy hollow wafer rolls filled with chocolate cream, often served as a nostalgic snack during family gatherings or celebrations.

name: Strawberry Roll
description: Crispy cylindrical wafer rolls with strawberry filling, a nostalgic treat often enjoyed with tea or shared with friends.

name: Cashewnut Cookie
description: Golden round cookie topped with cashew pieces, commonly enjoyed as a nostalgic homemade-style snack during festive seasons.

name: Walnut Cookie
description: Round biscuit with visible walnut pieces and a slightly crumbly texture, often eaten during festive celebrations or family tea sessions.

name: Yam Cookie
description: Small round cookie with a light golden colour and yam flavour, reminiscent of traditional nostalgic snacks enjoyed with tea.

name: Peanut Cookie
description: Round crumbly cookie topped with peanuts, a nostalgic festive snack often eaten during celebrations.

name: Chocolate Chip Cookie
description: Round cookie dotted with chocolate chips, bringing back nostalgic memories of homemade snacks enjoyed anytime.

name: Round Butter Cookie
description: Classic golden round butter cookie, often found in nostalgic biscuit tins and enjoyed during tea time.

name: Sweet Heart Cookie
description: Heart-shaped buttery cookie with a smooth surface, a nostalgic sweet treat often shared during gatherings or celebrations.

name: Daisy Cookie
description: Flower-shaped butter cookie with delicate petal edges, commonly enjoyed as a nostalgic festive snack.

name: Chocolate Cream Cookie
description: Round chocolate sandwich biscuits with cream filling, a nostalgic childhood snack often enjoyed with milk.

name: Cereal Rasin
description: Round biscuit with visible cereal flakes and raisins, reminiscent of nostalgic breakfast-style snacks eaten during tea breaks.

name: Oatmeal
description: Rustic round biscuit with visible oats, a nostalgic wholesome snack often enjoyed with tea or coffee.

name: Magic Dust Popping Candy
description: Tiny colourful candy crystals that pop and crackle in your mouth, often enjoyed as a fun nostalgic treat during childhood snack times or parties.

name: Mix and Match Sweets
description: Assorted colourful candies in different shapes and flavours, reminding many of nostalgic trips to the neighbourhood sweet shop where kids could pick their favourite treats.

name: Potato Wheel Crackers
description: Light crunchy crackers shaped like small wagon wheels, often eaten as a nostalgic savoury snack during school recess or afternoon breaks.

name: Toasted Seaweed Rings
description: Small ring-shaped crackers coated with savoury seaweed seasoning, a nostalgic snack often enjoyed while watching TV or sharing with friends.

name: Ice Pops
description: Long colourful frozen ice treats in plastic tubes, commonly enjoyed as a refreshing nostalgic snack on hot afternoons after school.

name: Cotton Candy
description: Fluffy pastel-colored spun sugar that melts in your mouth, often enjoyed at nostalgic funfairs and school carnivals.

name: Popcorn
description: Light and fluffy popped corn kernels, commonly shared during nostalgic movie nights or festive fairs.

name: Big Opak
description: Large round crispy cracker with a light and airy texture, often enjoyed as a nostalgic snack during family gatherings.

name: Tapioca Chips
description: Thin golden chips made from tapioca, a crunchy nostalgic snack often eaten during afternoon tea or festive visits.

name: Whistle Candy
description: Hard candy shaped like a small whistle that makes a sound when blown, a playful nostalgic treat popular among children during school breaks.

name: Biscuit Piring Wafer
description: Round wafer biscuits with light cream layers, often served as a nostalgic tea-time snack in traditional biscuit tins.

name: Eyeglass Candy
description: Colorful candy shaped like playful eyeglasses, a nostalgic novelty sweet often enjoyed by children after school.

name: Push Pop
description: Colorful lollipop in a push-up tube, a nostalgic sweet treat often shared among friends during school recess.

name: Muruku Sticks
description: Golden crunchy sticks made from dough, a nostalgic savoury snack commonly enjoyed during festive celebrations.

name: Peko Poko Chocolate
description: Popular stick-mounted lollipops featuring 3D molded faces, often remembered as a nostalgic childhood treat.

name: Baby Bottle JellyBean
description: Tiny jelly beans packed in a baby bottle-shaped container, a nostalgic candy often enjoyed during school snack times.

name: Animal Biscuit
description: Small crunchy biscuits shaped like animals, a nostalgic childhood snack often eaten during playtime or tea breaks.

name: Skisser Sour Candy
description: Colorful chewy candies coated with sour sugar, a nostalgic treat often enjoyed by children after school.

name: Dried Fish Fillet
description: Thin savoury strips of dried fish with a sweet and salty taste, a nostalgic snack often enjoyed during casual gatherings.

name: Kumquat Sticks
description: Sweet and tangy preserved kumquat strips, a nostalgic treat often eaten during festive visits or tea time.

name: Danhua Cake

name: Dippy Gummy

name: Chocolate Paste
description: Thick sweet chocolate spread usually packed in small tubes, a nostalgic treat often squeezed onto biscuits or bread during childhood.

name: Jelly Cup
description: Small colorful jelly desserts in plastic cups, a nostalgic sweet snack often enjoyed during parties or after-school treats.
